Question Text: In your own words, what issue concerns you the most?

I am going to name some issues, and I would like you to tell me if you believe the country is getting better, getting worse, or about the same as it was five years ago on these issues.... Providing quality public education
Reducing anti-Hispanic and anti-immigrant attitudes
Providing well-paying jobs or creating jobs
Treating all people with dignity and respect
Making sure affordable housing is available
Providing equal opportunities in education and workplace
Reducing crime and violence

Now take a moment to think about all the people in your family, your friends, co-workers, and other people you know.
Do you happen to know somebody who is an undocumented immigrant?
This is completely anonymous, and just for a simple demographic analysis.
Do you know a person or family member who faced detention or deportation for immigration reasons?

Mr. Garcia says Hispanics will have better opportunities if barriers to education and the workplace are broken down and Hispanics are encouraged and welcome to participate in all aspects of American life.
Mr. Garcia says including Hispanics and creating better opportunities will ultimately improve the lives of everyone.
Mr. Lopez says we have come a long way as a nation and there are enough opportunities for people who play by the rules.
Mr. Lopez thinks Hispanics will be better off if they put less emphasis on their cultural identity, such as language, food, and traditions and ignore barriers that exist.
Whom do you agree with the most? Mr. Garcia or Mr. Lopez?
To whom would you lean towards agreeing with the most, Garcia or Lopez?
